div.h1: Shell Process Memory Layout & Fork() Mechanics
div.p: Fork copies the virtual address space — COW (Copy-On-Write) defers physical page allocation until the first write.

g.text: Page Tables | ~4 KB/4 MB | Pointer copies
Virtual Memory | Private (COW) | Changes don't leak
Environment | Private (COW) | export x=1 only local
Work Directory | Private (PCB) | cd only affects child
File Offsets | SHARED (File) | lseek affects both
Signal Mask | Private (PCB) | Copied, then independent
Global Vars | Private (Data) | Internal shell state
read PATH | Read access | NO fault (shared)
FD Table is copied, but FILE OBJECTS are shared

// Break link to shared physical page

g.text: Note: pgid write triggers physical copy
